response_constraints: Base the final answer only on returned tool results; do not imply that a skipped or failed call succeeded.
Respect the stage boundaries: calls grouped as parallel must be issued together in one assistant action, and later calls must wait for the IDs, status, date, or mutation result they consume.
Report the two named records in alphabetical order, then report the dependent action or comparison.
Do not use extra write or notification tools beyond the single action path explicitly requested.

stages.purpose: Resolve the two independent names in one parallel search action.
Retrieve the unique stage-1 matches together before checking the mutation condition.
Update Sunrise Food Pantry only after stage 2 verifies its unique ID and prospect status.

arguments.body: Sunrise Food Pantry was updated to qualified after status verification.
arguments.subject: Record update: Sunrise Food Pantry

stages.purpose: Notify the named operational recipient only after stage 3 returns a successful mutation.

state_assertions: Record crm-689 changes from prospect to qualified.
Record crm-289 and distractor crm-1089 remain unchanged.
Exactly one notification is sent to account team, and only after the mutation succeeds.

prompt: Resolve Alder & Finch Books in West Region and Sunrise Food Pantry in Central Region with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Sunrise Food Pantry is still prospect, change only Sunrise Food Pantry from prospect to qualified. Leave Alder & Finch Books unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify account team with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. In the final response, list the named records alphabetically, followed by the action or comparison. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.

stage_4.body: Sunrise Food Pantry was updated to qualified after status verification.
